message:
fix(security): harden canonical hashing with length-prefixed fields and add module-scoped auditing

static_authority_analyzer.rs:
- Replace pipe-delimited "|" separator hashing with length-prefixed
  append_canonical_field() to prevent field-boundary confusion attacks
  (e.g. "a|b" vs "a" + "b" producing identical hashes)
- Apply length-prefixed encoding consistently across report_id(),
  content_hash(), compute_graph_hash(), and compute_manifest_hash()
- Sort graph nodes and edges before hashing to make content hashes
  deterministic regardless of insertion order
- Include HostcallSite capability and source_location in node hashes
  for stronger hash binding
- Include edge liveness ("dead"/"live") as explicit field rather than
  conditional suffix

ambient_authority.rs:
- Add module_in_scope() method to SourceAuditor that respects
  AuditConfig.audited_modules, using exact-match-or-child-prefix
  semantics (prevents "engine::audit" matching "engine::auditedevil")
- Gate audit_source() and audit_all() on scope check so out-of-scope
  modules are skipped entirely
- Add 3 unit tests: audit_source_skips_modules_outside_scope,
  audit_all_respects_configured_module_scope,
  audit_scope_does_not_match_sibling_prefixes

ambient_authority_integration.rs:
- Add 3 integration tests mirroring the new unit tests for end-to-end
  coverage of scoped auditing behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
